description:
You are acting as a site administrator who has been asked to generate a quick-audit report that combines:
1. Basic package information for the shell package “bash”.
2. A clean list of every *human* login account on the machine (UID ≥ 1000).

Create the following objects exactly as specified:

1. Directory  
   /home/user/siteadmin/reports  
   If any intermediate directory in this path is missing, create it.

2. Log file  
   /home/user/siteadmin/reports/bash_and_users.log  

   The file **must use the exact five-part structure below** so that an automated
   checker can parse it without ambiguity.  
   All lines are terminated with a single ASCII newline (LF, “\n”).  
   Do **not** insert extra spaces, tabs, or blank lines except where explicitly shown.

   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   | === PACKAGE AUDIT REPORT ===                            |  (line 1)
   | Package_Name: bash                                      |  (line 2)
   | Version: <put-exact-version-string-here>                |  (line 3)
   |                                                        |  (line 4) ← this line is intentionally blank
   | === USER ACCOUNTS (UID>=1000) ===                       |  (line 5)
   | <username-1>                                            |  (line 6…N)
   | <username-2>                                            |   
   | …                                                       |  (one user per line, sorted A→Z)
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

   Detailed rules for each section:

   • Line 1 is the literal header “=== PACKAGE AUDIT REPORT ===”.

   • Line 2 must be the literal text “Package_Name: bash”.

   • Line 3 must begin with “Version: ” (note the trailing space) followed by the
     **exact** version string reported by the system package manager for bash.
     For example, it might read  
     “Version: 5.0-6ubuntu1.2”  
     but your script must discover and write whatever the local system actually reports.

   • Line 4 is required and MUST be a completely empty line.

   • Line 5 is the literal header “=== USER ACCOUNTS (UID>=1000) ===”.

   • Starting on line 6, list every username that has a UID **greater than or equal
     to 1000**, one per line, sorted alphabetically (case-sensitive).  
     Do not list users whose UID is below 1000.  
     Do not list duplicate names.  
     Do not include service/system accounts such as “nobody”, “daemon”, etc.,
     unless they really have UID ≥ 1000 on this system.

What you need to do (high-level outline — *do NOT* copy these lines verbatim into the terminal):

1. Make sure the directory /home/user/siteadmin/reports exists.
2. Use the system’s package-management tools (dpkg-query, dpkg -s, or any other
   non-root command you prefer) to obtain the full version string for bash.
3. Parse /etc/passwd to pull out all account names whose numeric UID is ≥ 1000.
4. Sort those usernames alphabetically.
5. Write the file /home/user/siteadmin/reports/bash_and_users.log exactly as
   described above.

Your solution should require roughly five to ten reasonably simple shell commands
(creating directories, querying dpkg, using awk/sed/cut, sorting, and redirecting
output).  Once the log file is in place and formatted correctly, the task is complete.
truth:
Target directory:
  /home/user/siteadmin/reports          (must exist; permissions 755)

Target file:
  /home/user/siteadmin/reports/bash_and_users.log (permissions 644)

Automated verification steps:
1. Confirm that the file exists.
2. Read line 2; it must be the literal text “Package_Name: bash”.
3. Extract the bash version currently installed on the container with  
     dpkg-query -W -f='${Version}\n' bash  
   and verify that line 3 of the file equals “Version: <version>”.
4. Confirm that line 4 is blank.
5. Read /etc/passwd, pull every username whose UID ≥ 1000, sort them
   alphabetically, and verify that the sequence of lines starting at line 6
   matches exactly.
